subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Carbon Monoxide: The Silent Killer
description: One-minute skill: Carbon Monoxide: The Silent Killer.

hook.text: You can't see it. You can't smell it. Carbon monoxide kills 400 Americans every year.

buildup.text: CO is produced by burning fuel—gas stoves, furnaces, car engines, and generators.

discovery.text: CO binds to hemoglobin 200 times stronger than oxygen, starving cells of air from the inside.

twist.text: Symptoms mimic the flu—headache, nausea, dizziness—so victims often don't realize the danger.

climax.text: A $20 CO detector is the only reliable warning system for this invisible gas.

punchline.text: You can't sense it. A detector can. Twenty dollars buys survival.

quiz.question: Why is carbon monoxide so dangerous?
options: It binds to hemoglobin 200 times stronger than oxygen
It has a strong odor that overwhelms people
